meta_title: For Students: analytics setup to Solve no portfolio (Advanced Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome no portfolio. A Advanced-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Analytics Setup to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Learn how to implement professional analytics to overcome the 'no portfolio' hurdle. An advanced guide using Antigravity and Claude Code for AI builders.
content.answer_block: Transform your silent project into a data-driven case study. By integrating custom event tracking via Antigravity and automating implementation with Claude Code, you demonstrate professional-grade engineering skills. This turns a simple app into a measurable, high-impact portfolio piece that proves you understand user behavior and product-led growth.
content.intro:
The blank screen is the developer’s greatest enemy, but a finished app with zero usage data is the silent killer of your career prospects. You have spent weeks coding, yet your portfolio remains a collection of static repositories that tell hiring managers nothing about how real users interact with your logic.

It is time to bridge the gap between 'code that runs' and 'product that scales.' By treating your analytics setup as a core engineering feature rather than an afterthought, you transform your project from a student exercise into a professional case study. This guide leverages the raw power of Antigravity and Claude Code to give your work the narrative, data-backed authority it deserves.

steps.title: Define Your North Star Metrics
steps.description: Identify the specific user actions that validate your app's value proposition. Don't track everything; track what proves user success.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a Senior Product Engineer. Analyze my current codebase and suggest 3 high-value user events to track that demonstrate product-market fit for a portfolio case study.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --instruction 'Identify core user interaction points in the codebase and map them to standard analytics event schemas.'

steps.title: Architecting the Event Pipeline
steps.description: Create a decoupled analytics middleware to prevent your business logic from being polluted by tracking code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a clean, modular TypeScript middleware for capturing custom analytics events that can be easily swapped between providers.

steps.title: Automating Integration with Claude Code
steps.description: Deploy your tracking hooks across the application without manual boilerplate. Use Claude Code to ensure consistency.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a script that parses my component directory and injects the 'trackEvent' utility into all primary call-to-action buttons.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --instruction 'Scan src/components and automatically wrap onClick handlers with the trackEvent utility defined in step 2.'

steps.title: Implementing User Session Context
steps.description: Ensure every event carries metadata like session ID and user state to allow for funnel analysis in your portfolio breakdown.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor the analytics helper to automatically append session context and user properties to every outgoing event payload.

steps.title: Validating Data Flow
steps.description: Verify your events fire correctly in a staging environment before pushing to production.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a suite of integration tests that trigger user interactions and verify the correct JSON payload is sent to the analytics mock-endpoint.

pro_tips: Always track the 'Time-to-Value' metric; hiring managers love seeing that you care about user friction.
Use custom properties to track AI model latency; it proves you can optimize LLM performance in production.
Never hardcode keys; use a dedicated configuration provider managed by Antigravity's secret management patterns.
Visualize your event stream in your README using a screenshot of your analytics dashboard; it adds immediate credibility.
Implement a 'debug mode' flag that logs events to the console, allowing you to demonstrate your testing process during an interview.
resources: Analytics Best Practices for SaaS: https://segment.com/academy
content.next_steps: Once your analytics are live, create a 'Data Insights' section in your README. Highlight one specific user behavior trend you discovered and how you optimized your app based on that data.
content.conversion_block: You are no longer just a student; you are a builder with data-backed proof of your engineering capabilities. Stop waiting for permission to be an expert. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
